checked_must_haves: Runtime policy/model fields are additive and backward compatible
Local default path remains operational with explicit fallback behavior
Command branding is consistent as `portage` across docs, CLI help, and evidence scripts
Provider precedence works for macOS and Linux/WSL and is deterministic
Queue scheduling is bounded and queue lifecycle transitions are emitted
Diagnostics are user-visible for fallback and explicit-incompatibility failures
Operator bootstrap runbook exists and includes command validation sequence for Podman/Orbstack/Colima
Evidence capture includes named issue/template for recurring platform and evidence anomalies
checked_validation: Unit tests for resolver and compatibility matrix
Unit tests for factory/container runtime binary resolution
Integration tests for local/sandbox fallback and provider selection paths
Unit tests for queue admission and transition behavior
Portage runtime branding check passes in quickstart, migration guide, and evidence scripts

gates.name: release-hardening-checklist
gates.rationale: Hard gates must be explicit, checked, and mapped to run evidence.

gates.name: quickstart-help-replay
gates.rationale: Quickstart replay must include help command evidence markers.

gates.name: quickstart-local-sandbox-replay
gates.rationale: Quickstart replay must include local_sandbox execution evidence.

gates.name: quickstart-default-replay
gates.rationale: Quickstart replay must include default-mode execution evidence.

gates.name: quickstart-containerless-replay
gates.rationale: Quickstart replay must include containerless execution evidence.

gates.name: quickstart-queue-replay
gates.rationale: Quickstart replay must include queue execution and lifecycle evidence.

unresolved_risks: `microvm` and `wasi` are documented as research-track where platform support is not fully productionized in this release.
Full macOS/WSL end-to-end hardware matrix is environment-dependent and must be validated by operators in their own fleets.
Provider auto-installation is intentionally out of scope; admins must prepare runtime tools (Orbstack/Colima/Podman) before first run.
